title: A Big Twist in Space's Oldest Light
summary: Scientists are studying a strange twist in the oldest light in the universe, called the cosmic microwave background. This light is leftover from the Big Bang and helps us learn about space. They found that this light might be rotating slightly, which is called cosmic birefringence. This twist could be caused by tiny particles called axions. Researchers measured the twist angle and think it might be bigger than they thought before. They used a new method to make their measurements more accurate. This helps scientists understand dark matter and dark energy better. Future space experiments will use this method to learn more about the universe.

background_read: The universe started with a big explosion called the Big Bang billions of years ago. It left behind a faint light called the cosmic microwave background that scientists study to learn about space. Sometimes, this light has small twists that might tell us about invisible things like dark matter and dark energy. Scientists use special tools to measure these twists and make new discoveries about how the universe works.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from the University of Tokyo and Kavli IPMU
WHAT: They studied a twist in the oldest light called cosmic birefringence
WHERE: In space, using observations of the cosmic microwave background
WHY: To understand dark matter, dark energy, and test new physics ideas

perspectives.description: Scientists are excited because this twist could reveal new secrets about the universe.
